text: Feature branching is a core part of a typical version control workflow. When you start a new feature, you create a separate branch from the main codebase, often called "main" or "master". This keeps unstable code isolated while you work. After completing and testing the feature, you open a pull request. This allows teammates to review your changes before they are integrated. Finally, you perform a merging action, which combines your feature branch back into the main branch. This structured workflow prevents conflicts and ensures that the main code remains stable and deployable at all times.
